senses.definition: to eat small amounts or particular kinds of food, especially to lose weight

examples.x: She decided to diet by cutting out sugary snacks and eating more vegetables.
He started to diet to prepare for the upcoming marathon.
After the holidays, many people choose to diet to shed the extra pounds.
The doctor advised her to diet by following a low-carb plan.
They both decided to diet together, supporting each other through the process.

senses.definition: a specific eating plan tailored to manage blood glucose levels in individuals with diabetes

examples.x: Following a diabetic diet involves monitoring carbohydrate intake to regulate blood sugar levels.
Nutritionist guides on a personalized diabetic diet for balance.
Portion control education is key to a successful diabetic diet.
Education about portion control is an essential aspect of adopting a successful diabetic diet.
A well-structured diabetic diet includes a variety of nutrient-rich foods to support overall well-being.

senses.definition: a diet that primarily consists of soft or liquid food, often consumed with a spoon, suitable for individuals with difficulty chewing or swallowing

examples.x: Patients recovering from surgery may be prescribed a temporary spoon food diet to ease the healing process.
Spoon food is a practical choice for those with dental issues or jaw pain.
The elderly sometimes find comfort in easily prepared spoon food options.
A nutritionist may recommend a nutritious blend for a balanced spoon food diet.
Hospitals offer a variety of nutrient-packed options for patients on a spoon food regimen.

senses.definition: a diet consisting of easily chewed and digested food, often recommended for individuals with dental issues, post-surgery, or difficulty swallowing

examples.x: A soft diet is commonly prescribed for patients recovering from dental procedures.
After oral surgery, individuals are advised to follow a temporary soft diet to avoid irritation.
Athletes with jaw injuries may temporarily switch to a soft diet to accommodate their needs.
The nursing home caters to residents with diverse dietary requirements, including a specialized soft diet.
Soft diet recipes focus on meals that are gentle on the stomach and easy to chew.

senses.definition: the basic and commonly consumed foods that make up the majority of a person or animal's diet
a diet emphasizing whole grains, fruits, and vegetables while avoiding processed and refined foods

examples.x: The macrobiotic diet encourages the consumption of whole, unprocessed foods.
People exploring the macrobiotic diet aim for improved physical and mental well-being through mindful eating.
Adhering to a macrobiotic diet may involve limiting the intake of animal products and processed foods.
The cookbook's recipes, designed for the macrobiotic diet, offer a variety of nutritious options.
A macrobiotic diet, with its emphasis on balance, has gained popularity for promoting overall well-being.

senses.definition: a type of diet in which one avoids eating processed food to become healthier

examples.x: Clean eating involves consuming whole, minimally processed foods and avoiding refined sugars and artificial additives.
She adopted a clean eating approach, prioritizing fresh fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins in her daily meals.
Clean eating promotes a balanced diet that supports overall health and well-being, emphasizing nutrient-dense foods over empty calories.
Following a clean eating regimen, he felt more energized and experienced improvements in his digestion and sleep patterns.
The clean eating movement has gained popularity as people become more conscious of the connection between diet and health outcomes.

senses.definition: the unit used to measure the amount of energy that a food produces

examples.x: Consuming more calories than your body needs can lead to weight gain over time.
Many people track their daily calorie intake to manage their weight and maintain a healthy diet.
Exercise helps burn calories and can contribute to weight loss or weight maintenance.
Food labels often include information about the number of calories per serving to help consumers make informed choices about their diet.

senses.definition: a diet that includes a variety of foods in appropriate proportions to meet nutritional needs and promote overall health

examples.x: Educating children about the benefits of a balanced diet sets the foundation for healthy eating habits.
A balanced diet involves consuming fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains in appropriate portions.
Busy professionals often struggle to maintain a balanced diet due to time constraints and convenience.
Achieving a balanced diet requires mindful choices and awareness of nutritional content.
The nutritionist emphasized the importance of incorporating diverse food groups into a balanced diet.

senses.definition: the feeling of wanting food

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, her appetite was hearty, craving a substantial meal to replenish her energy.
Stress can affect appetite, causing some people to lose interest in food while others may seek comfort in eating.
The chef's specialty dishes sparked everyone's appetite with their tantalizing aromas and vibrant presentation.
The medication had side effects that decreased his appetite, leading to concerns about maintaining proper nutrition.
She had a healthy appetite for learning, always eager to explore new topics and expand her knowledge.

senses.definition: a substance such as a vitamin, protein, fat, etc. that is essential for good health and growth

examples.x: Fiber is a nutrient that aids in digestion.
Vitamin C is an important nutrient for boosting the immune system.
A balanced diet includes a variety of nutrients.
Protein is a vital nutrient for muscle repair.
Lack of certain nutrients can lead to health problems.

senses.definition: a numerical measure of an individual's body fat based on their weight and height, often used to assess and categorize body weight in relation to health

examples.x: The doctor calculated the patient's body mass index to evaluate their overall health.
Maintaining a healthy body mass index is associated with a lower risk of certain health conditions.
A high body mass index may indicate an increased likelihood of obesity-related health issues.
Athletes and trainers monitor changes in body mass index to optimize performance and training.
Regular exercise and a balanced diet contribute to achieving and maintaining a healthy body mass index.

senses.definition: someone who avoids eating meat

examples.x: She became a vegetarian after learning about the environmental and ethical implications of meat consumption.
The restaurant offers a variety of options for vegetarians, including salads, vegetable stir-fries, and tofu dishes.
He has been a vegetarian for years and enjoys experimenting with plant-based recipes at home.
As a vegetarian, she prefers to cook with plant-based ingredients such as tofu, beans, and lentils.
She has been a vegetarian for five years and feels healthier.

senses.definition: someone who does not eat fish, eggs, or meat, but consumes dairy products
someone who does not consume or use anything that is produced from animals, such as meat, milk, or eggs

examples.x: She became a vegan after learning about the ethical and environmental impact of animal agriculture.
She has been a vegan for several years and advocates for animal rights and environmental sustainability.
He became a vegan after learning about the health benefits of a plant-based diet.
The vegans at the potluck brought a variety of delicious plant-based dishes to share with everyone.
The vegans in the group shared tips and recipes for making vegan versions of their favorite dishes.

senses.definition: a person who abstains from consuming meat but still includes fish and other seafood in their diet
a person who primarily follows a plant-based diet but occasionally includes meat or other animal products in their meals
a person who primarily consumes fruits, nuts, seeds, and other plant-based foods
someone who is an expert in the field of diet and nutrition
a diet that limits the consumption of carbohydrates, particularly those found in grains and sugary foods

examples.x: He decided to try a low-carb diet to manage his blood sugar levels and improve his overall health.
The ketogenic diet is a popular example of a low-carb diet that emphasizes high fat intake and very low carbohydrate consumption.
She found success in losing weight by following a low-carb diet and tracking her daily carbohydrate intake.
The doctor recommended a low-carb diet to her patient as part of a comprehensive treatment plan for managing insulin resistance.

senses.definition: a diet that restricts and reduces the consumption of foods high in fats

examples.x: She decided to adopt a low-fat diet after her doctor advised her to make dietary changes to improve her cholesterol levels.
The nutritionist recommended a low-fat diet to her clients as part of their weight loss plan.
He found success in managing his heart health by following a low-fat diet and incorporating regular exercise into his routine.
The cookbook featured a variety of delicious recipes suitable for a low-fat diet, making healthy eating enjoyable and flavorful.
She noticed improvements in her energy levels and overall well-being after switching to a low-fat diet and increasing her intake of fruits and vegetables.

senses.definition: a diet that stops the consumption of gluten altogether

examples.x: She switched to a gluten-free diet after being diagnosed with celiac disease to manage her symptoms and promote intestinal healing.
He follows a gluten-free diet to alleviate digestive issues and skin irritations associated with gluten consumption.

senses.definition: a diet that restricts the intake of calories, often with the goal of weight loss or improving overall health

examples.x: Low-calorie diets typically involve consuming fewer calories than the body expends in daily activities.
Some choose a low-calorie diet not just for weight loss but also to promote a healthier lifestyle.
Transitioning to a low-calorie diet should be done gradually to avoid potential negative impacts on metabolism and energy levels.
The nutritionist recommended a carefully planned low-calorie diet for the patient with weight-related health concerns.
She embarked on a low-calorie diet to shed a few pounds before the upcoming event.

senses.definition: a diet that gains sudden popularity and often promises rapid weight loss through unconventional methods, but may lack scientific support or long-term effectiveness

examples.x: Celebrity endorsements sometimes contribute to the popularity of a particular fad diet.
Fad diets usually rely on restrictive eating patterns that may be difficult to maintain in the long run.
he appeal of a fad diet lies in its promise of rapid results, drawing in individuals seeking quick fixes.
Nutrition experts caution against the potential health risks associated with some extreme fad diets.

senses.definition: a diet plan which is high in protein and fat but very low in carbohydrates, developed by Dr. Robert Atkins
a diet that prioritizes foods rich in vitamins, with the goal of maintaining optimal health and preventing nutritional deficiencies

examples.x: She adopted a high-vitamin diet to ensure her body received essential nutrients for overall well-being.
A high-vitamin diet may help prevent certain health conditions associated with vitamin deficiencies.
The nutritionist recommended my mom a personalized high-vitamin diet to address specific vitamin deficiencies.
My athlete friends often incorporate a high-vitamin diet to support their immune system and enhance recovery.
Adopting a high-vitamin diet can contribute to vibrant skin, improved energy levels, and overall vitality.

senses.definition: a diet specifically designed to address and correct deficiencies in essential vitamins

examples.x: Following a vitamin-deficiency diet is crucial for individuals diagnosed with specific nutritional gaps.
A doctor may recommend a vitamin-deficiency diet to address symptoms associated with inadequate nutrient intake.
The tailored vitamin-deficiency diet aims to replenish nutrient stores and improve overall health.
Feeling fatigued, I embraced a vitamin-deficiency diet with leafy greens, nuts, and citrus fruits.
Sarah's doctor recommended a vitamin-deficiency diet including lean meats and fortified cereals to address her B12 deficiency.

senses.definition: a diet that emphasizes increased intake of protein-rich foods to support muscle growth, repair, and overall bodily functions

examples.x: Inspired by my fitness goals, I embraced a high-protein diet, incorporating lean meats and plant-based protein sources into my meals.
Planning meals with protein-rich sources like eggs, tofu, and dairy is crucial for a successful high-protein diet.
The bodybuilder adhered to a strict high-protein diet to meet the demands of intense training and muscle development.
As I explore the world of nutrition, I prioritize the balance of macronutrients in my high-protein diet for comprehensive nutritional well-being.
In my bodybuilding journey, a high-protein diet supports muscle development during intense training.

senses.definition: a diet that emphasizes low-calorie and easily digestible foods, often chosen for weight management, digestive comfort, or specific health conditions

examples.x: Following a light diet, I focus on salads, fruits, and lean proteins for a balanced and low-calorie approach.
Athletes during their rest days may opt for a light diet to give their digestive systems a break while maintaining nutrient intake.
Alex, recovering from an illness, gradually reintroduced solid foods through a gentle light diet to ease digestion.
Sarah, aiming for digestive ease, prefers a light diet consisting of soups, steamed vegetables, and gentle proteins.
The nutritionist recommended a temporary light diet to alleviate digestive discomfort and support overall well-being.

senses.definition: a diet composed entirely or primarily of liquids, often used for medical reasons, weight loss, or digestive rest

examples.x: After surgery, Lia had a liquid diet for healing.
My sister drinks broths and teas for a gentle liquid diet.
I had a liquid diet to ease digestion after being sick.
He follows a liquid diet for health reasons.

senses.definition: a diet that restricts the intake of salt, often recommended for individuals with hypertension or other health conditions

examples.x: To manage my blood pressure, I adopted a low-salt diet, reducing my sodium intake from processed foods.
Rick prioritizes a low-salt diet, favoring natural flavors with herbs and spices.
A nutritionist advised my dad to a low-salt diet to manage his salt-sensitive health condition.
For heart wellness, my sister shifted to a low-salt diet, choosing fresh, unprocessed foods.
Maria's commitment to health includes a conscious choice of a low-salt diet.

senses.definition: a diet focused on reducing overall calorie intake for the purpose of weight loss or management

examples.x: I started a reducing diet to shed a few pounds, emphasizing portion control and mindful eating.
Mark, aiming for weight management, adopted a reducing diet that includes balanced meals with controlled calories.
Following a reducing diet leads to weight loss over time.
The reducing diet includes fruits, vegetables, and lean proteins.
My brother, on a journey to lose weight, follows a reducing diet that combines healthy eating with regular exercise.

senses.definition: a diet that completely eliminates or significantly restricts the intake of salt, often recommended for individuals with specific health conditions such as hypertension or kidney problems

examples.x: matthew has chosen to follow a salt-free diet for her health.
The doctor recommended a salt-free diet to reduce blood pressure.
His commitment to a salt-free diet impressed the nutritionist.
Following a salt-free diet requires careful label reading.
The grocery list for a salt-free diet includes fresh produce and unsalted nuts.

senses.definition: a diet that involves avoiding or minimizing foods and beverages that can irritate or worsen stomach ulcers, typically focusing on mild, easily digestible foods

examples.x: The doctor recommended an ulcer diet that avoids spicy foods.
We are researching the best recipes for an ulcer diet.
Following an ulcer diet may help in reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
An ulcer diet emphasizes foods that are gentle on the stomach lining.
The nutritionist provided a comprehensive guide to the ulcer diet.

senses.definition: a diet where a person eats normally for five days a week and restricts their calorie intake for the other two days
a short-term, drastic and restrictive diet that is usually aimed at rapid weight loss but may not be sustainable or healthy in the long term
to completely or partially avoid eating or drinking for a period of time, especially because of religious reasons
a facility or program that offers structured activities, fitness routines, and nutritional guidance to help individuals achieve weight loss and overall wellness

examples.x: The fat farm offers a structured program with personalized meal plans.
The fat farm focuses on fitness activities and healthy eating habits.
Participants at the fat farm engage in daily exercise routines.
The celebrity endorsed the effectiveness of her experience at the fat farm.
We're considering sending our son to a fat farm to address his health concerns.

senses.definition: a dietary plan that emulates the eating habits of early humans by focusing on whole, unprocessed foods
(of a diet or food) completely or mainly consisting of plants
a type of diet that involves severe caloric restriction or prolonged periods of fasting, often with the goal of rapid weight loss
a weight loss program that involves consuming low-calorie meal replacement products to achieve rapid weight loss
